items.title: Spotlight on SIG Storage
items.summary:

moving forward. This time, we catch up with <strong><a href="https://github.com/kubernetes/community/tree/master/sig-storage">SIG
Storage</a></strong>, the group responsible
for per
items.reason: Kubernetes SIG Storage is a core component of the Kubernetes ecosystem; a dedicated wiki page would document its responsibilities, projects, and recent activities.

items.title: Docker joins the Athena coalition: a cross-industry collaboration for supply chain security
items.summary: The obvious takeaway from 2026's biggest incidents is that attackers are increasingly using AI to move fast. Docker's CISO, Mark Lechner, wrote about this shift and what every engineering team should do now. What worries us is that the bar is about to drop further. For most of the last decade, findi
items.reason: Docker’s participation in the Athena coalition addresses supply‑chain security, a high‑interest topic for a technical wiki.

items.title: Docker Hardened Images enhanced vulnerability scanning with Docker and Aikido
items.summary: Aikido now scans Docker Hardened Images (DHI) with built-in VEX support. Vulnerabilities that Docker has verified as non-exploitable drop out of the queue automatically, so developers spend their time on findings that actually matter. This post walks through what changed, why it matters, and how use
items.reason: The integration of Aikido scanning with Docker Hardened Images introduces new VEX‑based vulnerability handling that merits its own reference article.

items.title: 5 Software Supply Chain Security Best Practices for Development Teams
items.summary: Understanding software supply chain security is one thing. Putting it into practice across a real pipeline, with real deadlines and real constraints, is another. Most organizations recognize that their software supply chain is a growing attack surface, but translating that awareness into concrete, r
items.reason: A practical guide on software‑supply‑chain security best practices is valuable content for developers and security engineers.

items.title: What is AI Governance? Frameworks, Principles, and Best Practices
items.summary: AI agents are moving fast. According to our State of Agentic AI report, 60% of organizations already have AI agents in production, yet 40% cite security and compliance as the number-one barrier to scaling them further. And that gap between adoption and oversight is exactly where AI governance lives.
items.reason: AI governance frameworks and principles are emerging standards; a wiki entry would consolidate the concepts presented by Docker.

items.title: Hardened Images Explained: Fewer CVEs, Smaller Attack Surface
items.summary: When security teams scan their container environments for the first time, they often discover hundreds of known vulnerabilities, and almost none of them trace back to application code. The overwhelming majority come from packages that shipped with the base image: shells, compilers, debug utilities,
items.reason: Explanation of Docker Hardened Images, their reduced CVE surface, and usage guidance is directly relevant to container security documentation.

items.title: GitHub Copilot CLI for Beginners: Overview of common slash commands
items.summary:
GitHub Copilot CLI for Beginners: Learn how to use slash commands to control your terminal AI agent.
The post GitHub Copilot CLI for Beginners: Overview of common slash commands appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
items.reason: GitHub Copilot CLI slash‑command overview introduces a new interaction model for AI‑assisted terminals; worth documenting for developers.

items.title: Accelerating researchers and developers building multilingual AI with a new open dataset
items.summary:
A new repository-level dataset, published on GitHub under CC0-1.0, helps researchers and developers discover multilingual developer content across READMEs, issues, and pull requests.
The post Accelerating researchers and developers building multilingual AI with a new open dataset appeared first on T
items.reason: The multilingual AI dataset released on GitHub is a resource that developers may want to reference; a wiki page can describe its scope and usage.

items.title: How we made GitHub Copilot CLI more selective about delegation
items.summary:
Better orchestration, fewer handoffs, faster progress, without a single new knob.
The post How we made GitHub Copilot CLI more selective about delegation appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
items.reason: Details on how GitHub Copilot CLI improves delegation are technical and relevant for users customizing AI assistance.

items.title: GitHub availability report: May 2026
items.summary:
In May, we experienced nine incidents that resulted in degraded performance across GitHub services.
The post GitHub availability report: May 2026 appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
items.reason: Monthly availability reports provide operational insight; a summary page can track service reliability trends.

items.title: Making secret scanning more trustworthy: Reducing false positives at scale
items.summary:
Alerts are more trustworthy and actionable when noise is reduced. See how we improved the verification step with context-aware LLM reasoning.
The post Making secret scanning more trustworthy: Reducing false positives at scale appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
items.reason: Improvements to secret‑scanning accuracy involve LLM reasoning; documenting the methodology helps security teams understand false‑positive reduction.
